description: Causal rules for digital physical design (synthesis through signoff). Domain: small-to-medium standard-cell ASIC flows on open-source PDKs (Sky130, ASAP7, Nangate45) and commercial nodes by extension.

rules.statement: On 130nm-class technology, a small in-order RV32 core with M-extension and FF regfile synthesizes to roughly 110k-160k um² of standard-cell area; the dominant contributors are the multiplier and the regfile.
rules.rationale: Yosys+ABC synthesis logs and ORFS reference flows for Ibex/sky130hd consistently land in this range. Multiplier and 32x32 FF regfile dominate cell count.

rules.statement: In small in-order RV32 cores with M-extension, the multiplier output through the writeback mux into the regfile is one of the top combinational cones; partial-product/Booth-style XOR/XNOR density is the synth-time signature.
rules.rationale: Multipliers carry deeper logic than ALU/branch in small cores. Confirmed on Ibex by 3916 comb cells with 788 XOR/XNORs in our run #1.

rules.statement: In small in-order cores, the LSU response path is a thin handshake/format wrapper, not a deep combinational cone; LSU is rarely the synth-time critical path on its own.
rules.rationale: Surprise-derived rule from cognipd run #1: predicted LSU was a top critical cone; actual gate count was 812 cells, smaller than ALU. Generic 'memory paths are slow' prior was wrong here.

rules.statement: In RV32-with-RVC cores, the compressed-instruction decoder is a top-3 combinational cone (often >2000 cells), driven by wide priority muxing and sign-extension nests; this creates an IF-stage timing risk often missed on first review.
rules.rationale: Surprise-derived from cognipd run #1: ibex_compressed_decoder synthesized to 2568 comb cells, second only to multdiv_fast.

rules.statement: Post-synthesis HFN ranking starts with clock and reset; everything else is design-specific. Ranking the rest by gate count of the consuming logic predicts HFN better than control-vs-datapath labels.
rules.rationale: Confirmed in cognipd run #1: clk_i (839) > rst_ni (553) > datapath operands. Earlier 'control signals dominate HFN' rule was retired in favor of this.

rules.statement: A core with an FF-implemented register file and no SRAM memories has zero hard macros at floorplan; it is a pure stdcell floorplan.
rules.rationale: Definitional: a flop array is a stdcell array.

rules.statement: Core utilization at or below 50% on a small stdcell design provides ample routing margin; congestion is rarely the failure mode at this density.
rules.rationale: 65-70% is the routing-pressure threshold on most stdcell flows; 50% is well below.

rules.statement: Post-placement standard-cell count typically expands 10-15% beyond post-synth count due to buffering, sizing, and timing fixes during place-opt.
rules.rationale: Empirical observation across multiple PD flows; ratio varies with timing pressure.

rules.statement: Small cores at 50% utilization with no hard macros and a clean RTL pass detail-place with zero violations.
rules.rationale: Detail placement legalizes; low density and lack of macros remove the usual sources of legalization conflicts.

rules.statement: An FF-implemented register file (~1024 sinks for 32x32) inflates CTS leaf-buffer count well above what a small core would otherwise need, often pushing total clock-tree buffer count into the 1000-2000 range.
rules.rationale: 1024 flop clock pins force a deep balanced tree even on a tiny design.

rules.statement: Sky130-class clock-tree buffer trees add 0.3-0.5 ns of insertion-delay variance at the leaves; on a 10 ns budget this typically degrades post-CTS WNS by 0.3-0.7 ns versus pre-CTS.
rules.rationale: Sky130 stdcells have ~70-150 ps per buffer; a 4-5 deep tree accumulates the cited variance.

rules.statement: Small stdcell cores at 50% utilization with no macros and a non-flat clock tree route to zero detailed-route DRC errors on Sky130/Nangate.
rules.rationale: DRC failures correlate with congestion + macro-induced channel constraints; absent both, modern detailed routers close cleanly.

rules.statement: An Ibex-class small in-order RV32 core does not close timing at 100 MHz on Sky130HD with the default ORFS flow; expect setup WNS in [-0.7, -0.3] ns and hold WNS in [-0.6, 0.0] ns.
rules.rationale: ORFS rules-base.json publishes finish_wns >= -0.5 as the acceptable bound. Sky130 stdcell delays plus multdiv depth exceed the 10 ns budget.
